turns.content: What brings you in today?

I've been having a really tough time lately, doctor. I can't sleep through the night, and when I do wake up, my heart is just pounding in my chest before I've even opened my eyes. I'm a PhD candidate and I can't focus on my research at all anymore - it's like my brain is constantly racing.
When did this start?

It started about two and a half months ago. I remember it so clearly because I had a big presentation coming up for my dissertation proposal. I just thought it was normal stress at first, but it never went away - it's only gotten worse since then.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's not really a sharp pain - more like a tightness, a heavy pressure right here in my chest. Sometimes I feel like I can't take a deep enough breath, like something is sitting on my lungs. And my neck and shoulders are always tense, like I've been bracing for something bad to happen, even when I'm safe in my own bed.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills at all. But I have been waking up drenched in sweat some mornings, and my hands are always shaky and clammy for the first hour or so. It's really unsettling.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not on any prescription medications right now. I do take caffeine pills sometimes to try to get through long research days, but I've actually stopped those because I noticed they make the racing heart even worse. I've also tried melatonin gummies for sleep, but they didn't help much.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

No, I don't have any diagnosed chronic conditions. I mean, I had a thyroid panel done about a year ago during a routine checkup, and the doctor said it was borderline but not enough to treat. I haven't followed up on that since then, honestly.
Are you allergic to any medications?

No, I'm not allergic to any medications that I know of. I had penicillin once as a child and was fine. But honestly, doctor, I'm scared about what's happening to me. This isn't just stress anymore - it feels like my body is constantly in fight-or-flight mode, and I can't turn it off.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've tried everything I can think of. I cut out caffeine completely, I stopped working past 8 PM, I even downloaded meditation apps and tried breathing exercises before bed. Nothing helps. Some nights
Does anything make it better or worse?

My research actually makes it worse now, because I'm so anxious about not being able to focus that I just sit there staring at my screen, heart pounding. Meeting with my advisor or presenting anything sends
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's completely taken over my life, honestly. I used to be so productive and sharp, and now I can't even get through grading my students' papers without my mind wandering into a panic spiral. I'm isolating from my friends because I'm too exhausted and wound up to be social. I
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

I don't smoke at all. But I do drink wine, maybe a glass or two on weekends with friends. Although, recently I tried having one glass to see if it would help me relax and fall asleep, and it actually made my heart pound even harder. I haven't touched it since.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

Yes, actually. My mother has been on anti-anxiety medication for years, and she's always told me I'm just like her in that way. And my father had panic attacks in his late twenties - he described feeling like he was having a heart attack. I never really thought about it until now, but hearing myself speak, it feels like I'm telling their story.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

Just once, actually. In my senior year of undergrad, I had what I think was a panic attack before a final exam. My heart was racing, I felt dizzy and nauseous. But that was one time, and it went away after the exam ended. This feels
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say a 6 or 7. Not constant pain, but that tightness and racing heart are really wearing me out. Emotionally, though... I'm at a 9. I'm terrified every single day, and I feel like I'm losing control of my own body and my life. I can't keep living like this.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Just that I'm scared this is going to ruin my career before it even starts. I've worked so hard to get to this point, and now I can't even trust my own mind and body. I worry my advisor thinks I'm lazy or losing my edge. I didn't come here expecting to be told I'm fine - I know
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Honestly? I've just been white-knuckling through it. I show up to campus, pretend I'm okay in meetings, then come home and collapse into a heap of exhaustion and anxiety. I've stopped answering calls from friends because I don't want to admit how bad things are. I guess my only real coping mechanism has been avoidance, and I know that's not working.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've just been pushing through, faking it until I can't anymore. I'll cancel group meetings, avoid my lab, and then beat myself up for it later. I haven't let anyone in my life see how bad it really is, because I'm ashamed that I can't just handle this like a normal adult.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I haven't really been coping well at all, to be honest. I just avoid everything that triggers the panic - which is basically my entire research life right now. Sometimes I lie in bed in the dark and just wait for the adrenaline to pass, but it never fully does anymore.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm sorry, I feel like I keep repeating myself. I'm not coping, doctor. I'm just surviving hour to hour, and I'm exhausted from it. Every time I try something to make it better, it backfires, and I end up feeling even more trapped.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I don't think I'm coping at all, I'm just barely getting through each day. I'm sorry if I'm not answering your question the way you want, but I don't know what else to say. I came here because I need help, and I feel like I'm just repeating myself.
